message:
runtime_compat: add task::spawn_with_cx and unify cx-propagating spawn paths

Introduce `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx` on both the tokio and
asupersync sides so the single canonical API to "spawn a task with an
explicit Cx" lives in `runtime_compat`, mirroring how `task::spawn` /
`task::spawn_blocking` are already abstracted across the two runtimes.

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs
  - tokio branch: `spawn_with_cx` takes an `&Cx` but relies on the
    ambient request-Cx machinery (`crate::cx::for_request()`) to
    produce the child Cx, then delegates to `tokio::spawn`. The
    signature matches the asupersync variant so callers need not
    gate the call site.
  - asupersync branch: grabs the thread-local asupersync `Handle`
    (same helper used by `spawn`), clones the parent Cx, wraps the
    future in `HandleContextFuture` (so the child Cx is installed
    for the task's lifetime), and calls `handle.spawn(wrapped)`.
    Returns the same `JoinHandle { inner, aborted, abort_waker }`
    as the existing `spawn` path, preserving abort semantics.
  - New test `task_spawn_with_cx_receives_explicit_context`
    (asupersync-only) confirms the spawned task observes exactly the
    passed-in cx as its current cx and that a runtime handle is
    installed while the task runs.

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs
  - `spawn_workflow_child_task` (or equivalent helper) now calls
    `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx(runtime_cx, task_fn)`
    instead of cloning the Cx and delegating to `task::spawn` by
    hand. Drops the now-unused `task::{self}` glob re-import;
    only `task::JoinHandle` is still needed.

* crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs
  - `spawn_workflow_task` collapses the prior
    "if let Some(handle) = current_runtime_handle() { cx::spawn_with_cx } else { task::spawn }"
    split into a single call to `runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`.
    That helper internally handles the "no runtime handle installed"
    case on the asupersync side (via the `ASUPERSYNC_HANDLE`
    thread-local expect) and on the tokio side (where the helper
    never needs a runtime handle lookup).

Net effect: workflow/child-task spawns go through one path per runtime,
child Cx installation is guaranteed, and the call sites no longer need
to know which runtime they're compiled against.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
